task.title: Add markdown-validator to a GitHub Actions workflow
task.shortdesc: Create a GitHub Actions workflow file that installs markdown-validator, downloads NLTK language data, validates your documentation on every pull request, and uploads reports as downloadable artifacts.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Add markdown-validator as a GitHub Actions job to automatically validate Markdown files on every pull request and block merges on Required failures.

ul.li: Your documentation repository is hosted on GitHub.
The rule set file is committed to the repository.
You have write access to the repository to create workflow files.

context.p: A GitHub Actions workflow runs automatically when you open or update a pull request. If the validation step exits with code 1 (one or more Required rules failed), GitHub marks the check as failed and prevents the merge. Authors can see the report in the Actions log or as a downloaded artifact.

step.cmd: In your repository, create the directory .github/workflows/ if it does not exist.
Create a new file named .github/workflows/validate-docs.yml and paste in the following workflow. Do not modify it yet — update the two project-specific values in the next step.

info.codeblock:
name: Validate documentation
on: pull_request: paths: - 'docs/**/*.md'
jobs: validate: runs-on: ubuntu-latest
steps: - name: Check out repository uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Set up Python uses: actions/setup-python@v5 with: python-version: '3.12'
- name: Cache NLTK data uses: actions/cache@v4 with: path: ~/nltk_data key: nltk-punkt-tagger
- name: Install markdown-validator run: pip install markdown-validator
- name: Download NLTK language data run: | python -m nltk.downloader punkt_tab averaged_perceptron_tagger_eng
- name: Validate documentation run: | md-validate validate docs/ \ --rules rules/tutorial.json \ --format json \ --output reports/
- name: Upload validation reports if: always() uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: validation-reports path: reports/

step.cmd: Review the non-obvious workflow steps before customizing.

dlentry.dd: Caches the NLTK corpora between runs so the pipeline does not re-download them on every pull request.
Downloads punkt_tab and averaged_perceptron_tagger_eng for sentence-count and part-of-speech rules. Runs even when the cache is warm; the downloader skips files already present.
Exits with code 1 if any Required rule fails, marking the GitHub check as failed and blocking the merge.
if: always() ensures the upload runs even when validation fails, so authors can download the report without scrolling CI logs.

step.cmd: Update the workflow to match your repository's paths and rule set location.

ul.li: docs/**/*.md — the glob pattern matching your documentation files
docs/ (in the validate command) — the directory to scan
rules/tutorial.json — the path to your team's rule set file

step.cmd: Commit the workflow file and push the branch.

info.codeblock: git add .github/workflows/validate-docs.yml git commit -m "Add documentation validation workflow" git push

step.stepresult: GitHub runs the workflow on every subsequent pull request that changes files matching the path filter.

step.cmd: Open a pull request to verify the workflow runs correctly.

info.p: Navigate to the Actions tab of your repository and select the latest workflow run to see the validation output. If the workflow fails, the output shows which rules failed and what to fix.
